description: Scott Cawthon's indie horror phenomenon — a security guard simulator turned sprawling multimedia empire about haunted animatronics, missing children, and one of gaming's most obsessively documented lore puzzles. Launched 2014, never stopped releasing content.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's
media.description: The original. Monitor cameras, manage doors and lights, survive five nights against four malfunctioning animatronics at Freddy Fazbear's Pizza.
media.notes: PC (Steam/itch.io). Mobile port available iOS/Android. Console ports via Steel Wool.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's 2
media.description: Prequel set in a newer (and worse) Freddy Fazbear's. No doors — flashlight and a Freddy mask are your only defenses. Introduces the Withered animatronics and Toy variants.
media.notes: Released just 2 months after FNaF 1. Sets much of the core lore.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's 3
media.description: Fazbear's Fright horror attraction, 30 years later. One animatronic threat (Springtrap) plus phantom hallucinations. The minigames tell the darkest chapter of the lore.
media.notes: Has a true ending and a bad ending. The minigames are arguably the most important story content in the series.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's 4
media.description: No cameras. A child alone in a house, guarding bedroom doors and closet against Nightmare animatronics. The most atmospheric entry. Lore implications heavily debated.
media.notes: The '87 minigames and the Fredbear Bite subplot. Released Halloween DLC adding Nightmare BB, Jack-O variants, and others.

media.title: FNaF World
media.description: Chibi-art RPG starring the animatronics. Originally released in broken state and pulled from Steam; repatched and made free. Hidden lore connects back to the main series via Fredbear dialogue.
media.notes: Available free on GameJolt. Considered soft-canon — the secret endings and Fredbear text are considered meaningful by many lore analysts.

media.title: FNaF World: Halloween Edition
media.description: Standalone Halloween reskin of FNaF World with all-new Halloween-themed characters. Free release.
media.notes: Free on GameJolt. Side content.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Sister Location
media.description: Circus Baby's Entertainment and Rental — an underground facility with a new cast. Fully scripted narrative, technician premise. Introduces Baby, Ballora, Funtime Freddy, Ennard. Major story pivot.
media.notes: Custom Night DLC adds the 'fake ending' that contradicts the main ending intentionally. Baby becomes a major series character.

media.title: Freddy Fazbear's Pizzeria Simulator
media.description: Presented as a free tycoon game on Steam. Actually FNaF 6 — the conclusion of the Afton/Emily arc. Salvage damaged animatronics and run a new pizzeria by night. Ending is a genuine payoff.
media.notes: Free on Steam. The ending is widely considered one of the best in the franchise. Don't spoil it.

media.title: Ultimate Custom Night
media.description: 50 animatronics. Mix-and-match difficulty dials. More a boss-rush victory lap than a story entry — but the final cutscene is canon and major.
media.notes: Free on Steam. The 50/20 mode is legendary in the community. The Cassette Man/Afton ending matters.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's VR: Help Wanted
media.description: VR anthology remaking FNaF 1–4 minigames plus new content. First entry developed by Steel Wool under Cawthon supervision. Introduces Tape Girl, the Glitchtrap storyline, and the Mimic concept.
media.notes: Non-VR flat mode available. Glitchtrap (William Afton digitised) is the major new lore element. Won multiple VR awards.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's AR: Special Delivery
media.description: Augmented reality mobile game — animatronics delivered to your real-world location, encountered through your phone camera. Operational until January 2023 shutdown.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers shut down January 13, 2023. No longer playable. Community-captured footage exists.

media.title: Freddy in Space 2
media.description: Side-scrolling shooter released as a charity fundraiser for Extra Life. Freddy shoots through space. Not a main series entry.
media.notes: Free on GameJolt. Charity release. Non-canon.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's VR: Help Wanted — Curse of Dreadbear
media.description: Halloween DLC for Help Wanted. Dreadbear (a Frankenstein-Freddy) hosting a haunted maze of challenges. Primarily aesthetic, some lore.
media.notes: Paid DLC for Help Wanted.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Help Wanted 2
media.description: VR sequel with all-new minigame anthology, including content based on Security Breach, Ruin, and prior games. Continues the Mimic lore thread.
media.notes: PSVR2 and PC (Steam VR). Flat mode available. Mimic story progression.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Security Breach
media.description: Open-world stealth/exploration in a massive Freddy Fazbear Mega Pizzaplex. Gregory and Glamrock Freddy. Multiple endings. Massive departure in tone and design from classic entries.
media.notes: Major performance issues at launch, substantially patched. Ruin DLC is essentially a corrective sequel. Many endings are non-canon.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Security Breach — Ruin
media.description: Free DLC campaign for Security Breach. Cassie enters the abandoned Pizzaplex searching for Gregory. The Mimic is formally introduced. Considered the actual narrative capstone of the Mimic arc.
media.notes: Free DLC. The Mimic's reveal here connects Help Wanted 1, Help Wanted 2, and the Tales books.

media.title: Freddy in Space 3: Chica in Space
media.description: Third charity game in the Freddy in Space series. Side-scroller featuring Glamrock Chica. Released for Extra Life charity.
media.notes: Free on GameJolt. Non-canon charity release.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Into the Pit
media.description: Adaptation of the 'Into the Pit' short story from Fazbear Frights. 2D pixel adventure — Oswald falls through a ball pit into 1985. Springtrap in his prime. Critically acclaimed.
media.notes: Developed by Mega Cat, published by Maximum Entertainment. Based directly on the anthology story.

media.title: One Night at Flumpty's
media.description: Fan game / spiritual parody. An egg named Flumpty kidnaps you. One of the earliest and most beloved FNaF fan games; eventually received official acknowledgment and a full series.
media.notes: Fan-made. Available free. The Flumpty trilogy (ONaF 1–3) became an ascended fan series — Scott Cawthon personally promoted it.

media.title: One Night at Flumpty's 2
media.description: Sequel with expanded cast and more complex mechanics. Owl, Clown, Grunkfuss the Clown, and Birthday Boy Blam return.
media.notes: Fan-made, widely regarded as better than the original. Available free.

media.title: One Night at Flumpty's 3
media.description: Long-awaited conclusion. Fully remastered graphics and sound. Commercially released on mobile (paid). Received a trailer on the official FNaF social media — extremely ascended for a fan game.
media.notes: Available on iOS/Android (paid). PC free on itch. Scott Cawthon publicly promoted this one.

media.title: The Joy of Creation: Story Mode
media.description: Fan game where Scott Cawthon's own family is stalked by a demonic version of Ignited Freddy. Narrative-driven, multiple chapters, each with different mechanics. One of the most acclaimed FNaF fan games ever made.
media.notes: Free on GameJolt. Not canon to the main series but widely considered a must-play. Ignited animatronics became iconic in the community.

media.title: Five Nights at Candy's
media.description: Fan game set in a different restaurant — Candy's Burgers & Fries. One of the most polished and feature-complete FNaF fan games, with its own expanded lore and sequels.
media.notes: Free. Has three entries (FNaC 1–3) plus a remaster. Entirely self-contained lore.

media.title: POPGOES
media.description: Fan game set at the POPGOES Pizzeria. Popgoes the Weasel and his animatronic family. Well-regarded for puzzle mechanics and unusually deep original lore. Received a full remake (POPGOES Evergreen) in 2023.
media.notes: Free. POPGOES Evergreen (2023) is the recommended version. Kane Carter is now an official FNaF extended-universe contributor.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Novel
media.description: The original novelization by Scott Cawthon and Kira Breed-Wrisley. Mike Schmidt, a security guard at Freddy's, investigates the murders. Not a direct retelling — alternate continuity with original plot.
media.notes: Part of the Silver Eyes trilogy. This is the first book.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Twisted Ones
media.description: Second novel. Charlie confronts a new threat — the Twisted animatronics, built to create illusions and hunt outside. Introduces new lore about the technology behind the suits.
media.notes: Part of the Silver Eyes trilogy. Has a graphic novel adaptation (2019).

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Fourth Closet
media.description: Conclusion of the Silver Eyes trilogy. The true nature of Charlie is revealed. Major twists and a definitive end for the trilogy's continuity.
media.notes: Graphic novel adaptation released 2020.

media.title: Fazbear Frights #1: Into the Pit
media.description: First anthology short story collection. Three stories: 'Into the Pit' (Springtrap origin tie-in), '1:35 AM' (Fetch the robot dog), 'Out of Stock' (sentient plushies). Directly canon to the main games.
media.notes: Into the Pit adapted as the 2024 game. These anthologies are considered main-series canon.

media.title: Fazbear Frights #2–12 (Full Series)
media.description: Books 2 through 12 of the anthology series (Fetch, 1:35AM, Step Closer, Bunny Call, Blackbird, Lonely Freddy, The Cliffs, Gumdrop Angel, The Puppet Carver, Friendly Face, Bobbiedots). Mix of standalone horror and series-relevant lore.
media.notes: All Fazbear Frights entries are considered canon unless contradicted by games. The Mimic is introduced in 'Gumdrop Angel.'

media.title: Tales from the Pizzaplex #1–8 (Full Series)
media.description: Second anthology series set in the Pizzaplex era, running 8 volumes. Expands on Security Breach lore, Glamrock era characters, and the Mimic. 'The Mimic' story in vol. 2 is foundational for understanding Ruin.
media.notes: The Mimic story in Tales vol. 2 is required reading to fully understand Help Wanted 2 and Ruin. Directly canon.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Silver Eyes (Graphic Novel)
media.description: Graphic novel adaptation of the first novel, illustrated by Claudia Schröder. Condensed and visually adapted.
media.notes: Alternate continuity to the games. Graphic novel format.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Twisted Ones (Graphic Novel)
media.description: Graphic novel adaptation of The Twisted Ones.
media.notes: Same alternate continuity. Illustrated by Claudia Schröder.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Fourth Closet (Graphic Novel)
media.description: Graphic novel adaptation of The Fourth Closet.
media.notes: Completes the graphic novel trilogy. Illustrated by Claudia Schröder.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Movie
media.description: Universal/Blumhouse theatrical film. Mike Schmidt takes a night security job at the closed Freddy's to maintain custody of his sister Abby. Josh Hutcherson, Elizabeth Lail, Matthew Lillard. Partially based on Silver Eyes novel continuity.
media.notes: Box office success ($297M on $20M budget). Peacock day-and-date release. Sequel in development. Not strictly canon to the games.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Security Breach (Animated Short — RUIN Trailer)
media.description: Animated short film used as the Ruin DLC announcement trailer. Cassie and the Mask. Counts as a produced short animated work by Steel Wool.
media.notes: Official promotional animated content, not a standalone release.

media.title: Chipper & Sons Lumber Co. (Scott Cawthon early work)
media.description: Cawthon's pre-FNaF game, notable because review criticism of its 'terrifying' character models directly inspired him to lean into horror — leading to FNaF 1.
media.notes: Not a FNaF game. Historical context only. Free.

media.title: Scottgames Teasers / ARG Materials (2014–2021)
media.description: The scottgames.com website hosted a series of ARG-style teaser images between game releases, many containing hidden messages, glitch effects, and lore hints that the community decoded. Not interactive games, but an integral part of the FNaF experience.
media.notes: Archived community decode records exist on the FNaF wiki. Site no longer actively maintained.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's Plus
media.description: Fan-made remake of FNaF 1 with overhauled visuals, new mechanics, and additional lore. Initially just a fan project; Scott Cawthon sponsored and promoted it, making it semi-official.
media.notes: Available on GameJolt (free) and Steam (paid). Scott Cawthon's official promotion makes this an ascended fan work.

media.title: FNAF Simulator (Scott Cawthon's original prototype)
media.description: The original early prototype of FNaF, shared in limited form before the released game. Historical artifact.
media.notes: Archived on the internet. Not an official release.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: Security Breach RUIN (Official Animated Shorts)
media.description: Series of short animated clips produced by Steel Wool for Security Breach and Ruin trailers — includes the RUIN animated teaser and character reveal shorts.
media.notes: Official Steel Wool productions. Available on YouTube.

media.title: Five Nights at Freddy's: The Musical (Random Encounters)
media.description: Fan-made YouTube musical series by Random Encounters covering FNaF 1 through Sister Location. Enormously popular — several videos exceeded 100M views. Considered the quintessential early fandom artifact.
media.notes: Fan-made. Not canon. But culturally enormous for the fandom.

media.title: FNAF: The Series (fan animated — various creators)
media.description: The broader ecosystem of fan animated FNaF series on YouTube — including works by Smike, The Freddy Files, and numerous SFM animation channels. The community animation scene was one of the largest on YouTube 2014–2017.
media.notes: Not canon. Cultural documentation only.
